seoTitle: Thorngate vs Thornlea — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Thorngate and Thornlea. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Thorngate and Thornlea.
heading: Thorngate vs Thornlea — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Thorngate, South Australia and Thornlea, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Thornlea has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Thorngate (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Thorngate or Thornlea safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Thorngate has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Thornlea scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Thorngate scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Thornlea scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Thorngate or Thornlea?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Thornlea has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
